title: scrophularia auriculata subsp. valentina
intro: Scrophularia auriculata subsp. valentina, also known as Valentina's figwort, is a subspecies of the Scrophularia auriculata plant, which belongs to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits associated with Scrophularia auriculata subsp. valentina include reducing fever, relieving cough and respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
